title: Creating & Managing Deals
description: Learn how to create new deals, view deal details, update status, switch between grid and table views, and filter your deal pipeline.

steps.narration: Your deals pipeline is the command center for all active engagements. Here you can see your open deals at a glance, each card showing the company, deal name, status, and owner.
Let's create a new deal. Click the New Deal button in the upper right to open the creation form.
Fill in the deal details. Start with the company name and deal name. The form also lets you set the industry, assign a salesperson, and add a customer logo.
Once a deal is created, you land on its overview page. Here's the Meridian Dynamics Q2 Digital Transformation Initiative with its status, owner, and industry displayed in the header.
The metrics cards track your deal progress at a glance. Touches completed, days in pipeline, last activity date, and total interactions help you gauge momentum without digging into details.

callout.text: Key deal metrics at a glance

steps.narration: Below the metrics, the activity timeline shows every interaction with this deal. Pre-call briefings, touch sessions, and their completion status all appear here in chronological order.
Back on the deals page, you can see your full pipeline. The grid view gives a visual overview with cards for each deal showing the key details.
Switch to table view for a more compact layout. The view toggle lets you choose between the visual grid and a traditional table with sortable columns.
Table view lists all your deals with columns for company, deal name, status, owner, collaborators, last activity, and progress. This is ideal for comparing details across many deals at once.
Use the status filter tabs to narrow your view. Here we've filtered to show only won deals, so you can review which engagements have closed successfully.
Filtering to lost deals helps you review what didn't work out. Understanding patterns in lost deals can inform your approach on future opportunities.
Switch back to the all status filter to see your complete pipeline. The deals page gives you everything you need to manage your active engagements in one place.
